description: Agent skills, reusable prompt workflows, capability packs, and installable AI assistant extensions.

items.name: Guizang PPT Skill
items.summary: A Claude Code / Codex compatible agent skill for generating single-file HTML presentation decks, PPT visuals, and social cover images in editorial magazine or Swiss design styles.
use_cases: Generate horizontal swipe HTML slide decks from articles, Markdown, outlines, or product analysis notes.
Create presentation visuals, screenshots, and social cover images with consistent visual systems.
Use stricter Swiss-style layout validation for data, product, method, and technical presentations.

links.note: Usage, installation, supported platforms, and examples.
Main skill workflow and operating instructions.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-14.
Repository license is AGPL-3.0; review implications before adapting or redistributing generated/templates code.
Installation requires running npx or cloning a third-party repository into an agent skills directory.
The workflow expects an agent with local filesystem and shell access, so review source before granting broad permissions.
Generated HTML decks may use browser runtime features, local assets, WebGL/canvas, and possible CDN fallbacks; inspect output before sharing externally.
Presentation source materials, screenshots, and generated images may contain sensitive business or personal information.

source_review.github_description: AI-agent Skill for generating polished HTML slide decks: editorial magazine and Swiss layouts, image prompts, social covers, and a WebGL/low-power presentation runtime.

items.name: Huashu Design
items.summary: An agent-agnostic HTML-native design skill for producing high-fidelity prototypes, HTML slide decks, motion demos, infographics, design variations, expert reviews, and exportable MP4/GIF/PPTX/PDF artifacts.
use_cases: Create high-fidelity HTML app or web prototypes with clickable flows and browser verification.
Generate HTML-native presentation decks and optionally export them to editable PPTX or PDF.
Produce timeline-driven motion design demos and export MP4/GIF outputs with optional audio.
Explore multiple visual directions, apply brand asset protocols, and run structured design reviews.

links.note: Chinese README with positioning, installation, demos, and license notes.
Main agent workflow and operating instructions.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-14.
Installation requires running npx or cloning a third-party repository into an agent skills environment.
The skill expects local filesystem, shell, browser, Playwright, export scripts, and optional media-processing workflows.
Generated prototypes, decks, videos, and screenshots may embed private brand assets, product screenshots, customer data, or local file paths.
Some workflows involve downloading brand assets, public images, audio, or external references; source and licensing should be checked per project.
Optional voiceover or API-driven workflows may require secrets in environment variables; never commit generated .env files.
Repository is large and asset-heavy; evaluate install size and dependency surface before making it active.

source_review.github_description: Huashu Design · HTML-native design skill for Claude Code · Claude Code 里 HTML 原生的设计 skill · 高保真原型 / 幻灯片 / 动画 + 20 设计哲学 + 5 维评审 + MP4 导出 · Agent-agnostic

items.name: Superpowers
items.summary: A multi-skill software development methodology and cross-agent plugin package for coding agents, covering brainstorming, planning, TDD, systematic debugging, code review, worktrees, and subagent-driven development.
use_cases: Give coding agents a structured end-to-end development workflow from idea clarification through branch completion.
Enforce test-driven development, systematic debugging, code review, and verification-before-completion practices.
Install a common skills library across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Factory Droid, and GitHub Copilot CLI.
Use subagent-driven development and parallel-agent workflows for larger implementation plans.

links.note: Overview, installation instructions, workflow, and skill list.
Original release announcement linked from README.

install.codex: Install Superpowers from the official Codex plugin marketplace UI.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-15.
This is a behavior-shaping methodology package, not just a passive reference; it can strongly change how an agent plans, asks questions, writes tests, reviews code, and finishes branches.
The using-superpowers skill tells agents to invoke relevant skills before any response or action; verify this matches the desired workflow before enabling.
Repository includes hooks, plugin manifests, agent instructions, tests, scripts, and cross-platform adapter files; review install target and generated files per harness.
TDD and subagent-driven workflows can be valuable but may add process overhead for small tasks or conflict with existing project conventions.
User instructions are documented as higher priority than Superpowers skills, but local agent behavior should be tested before active use.
Installing into official plugin marketplaces or agent skill directories can affect future sessions; start in a disposable or project-local context.

items.name: Understand Anything
items.summary: A cross-platform agent plugin and skill suite that analyzes codebases, documentation, or knowledge bases into interactive knowledge graphs with dashboard exploration, chat, diffs, explanations, onboarding guides, and domain views.
use_cases: Analyze a large codebase into a searchable interactive knowledge graph for onboarding and architecture exploration.
Ask questions about codebase structure, flows, files, functions, classes, and dependencies after graph generation.
Generate dashboard views, guided tours, onboarding guides, diff impact analysis, and file/function explanations.
Analyze Karpathy-pattern LLM wiki knowledge bases into navigable knowledge graphs.
Use localized graph and dashboard content for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Russian, and English workflows.

links.note: Overview, features, commands, and install instructions.
Security reporting and local-only scope statement.

installer_behavior_observed: Clones the repository to ~/.understand-anything/repo by default.
For Codex, links per-skill folders into ~/.agents/skills.
Generated project analysis is written under .understand-anything/.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-15.
The installer is a curl-pipe shell script; review the script or clone manually before running it.
The plugin scans local projects and reads source files, docs, knowledge bases, file paths, imports, classes, functions, and dependency structure.
Generated graph data is written to .understand-anything/ and may contain sensitive architecture, business logic, filenames, summaries, and code-derived metadata.
README suggests committing generated graph JSON for team sharing; do this only after reviewing for secrets, private paths, proprietary code summaries, and business-sensitive information.
The tool uses LLM-agent analysis and static analysis; review whether analyzed source or summaries are sent to model providers by the active agent/harness.
Security.md states the tool is local-only and does not phone home, but local harness/model behavior and generated dashboard exposure still need verification before active use.
The dashboard includes a file-content endpoint gated by an access token and graph-derived path allowlist; verify before opening on shared networks.
Auto-update via post-commit hook can change repository behavior; enable only after reviewing hook files.

source_review.github_description: Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.

items.name: Qiaomu Anything to NotebookLM
items.summary: A Claude Code Skill for collecting multi-source content, uploading it to Google NotebookLM, and generating podcasts, slide decks, mind maps, quizzes, reports, Feishu documents, and other NotebookLM outputs.
use_cases: Convert WeChat articles, web pages, PDFs, EPUBs, Markdown, Office files, images, audio, ZIP files, and search results into NotebookLM sources.
Use NotebookLM to generate podcasts, PPT-style slide decks, mind maps, quizzes, reports, infographics, data tables, and flashcards.
Run deeper recursive-question analysis over long documents or media transcripts.
Create Feishu documents from NotebookLM analysis output when the Feishu integration is configured.

links.note: Overview, supported sources, install flow, examples, and paywall-bypass description.
Main Claude Skill instructions and source-handling workflow.
Shell installer that installs Python dependencies, Playwright Chromium, notebooklm-py, and a WeChat MCP server.

installer_behavior_observed: Clones Bwkyd/wexin-read-mcp into the skill directory when missing.
Installs Python dependencies from both the MCP server and skill requirements files.
Installs notebooklm-py from https://github.com/teng-lin/notebooklm-py.git when notebooklm is missing.
Prints a Claude MCP config snippet for the weixin-reader server.
Requires a separate NotebookLM browser/account login.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-15.
The repository explicitly implements paywall-bypass behavior for many news and media sites; this may violate site terms, copyright, subscription agreements, or local law.
The skill can collect, transform, and upload third-party content to NotebookLM/Google services; review copyright, privacy, and data-handling constraints before use.
The install script runs pip installs, installs Playwright Chromium, clones an MCP server, and installs notebooklm-py from GitHub; review the source and use a disposable environment before running it.
WeChat article access uses an MCP/browser-simulation workflow and should be reviewed before granting local browser, account, or filesystem access.
Optional Get笔记 API integration uses GETNOTE_API_KEY and GETNOTE_CLIENT_ID; keep these secrets out of the repository and shell history where possible.
The workflow may write outputs to /tmp, Feishu documents, and NotebookLM notebooks, which can expose sensitive source material or generated summaries.
The SKILL instructions say YouTube URLs should be passed directly to NotebookLM and not downloaded with yt-dlp, Whisper, or browser subtitle extraction.
Proxy services such as r.jina.ai, defuddle.md, archive.today, and other fetch methods may send requested URLs or content through third-party infrastructure.

source_review.github_description: Claude Skill: Multi-source content processor for NotebookLM. Supports WeChat articles, web pages, YouTube, PDF, Markdown, search queries → Podcast/PPT/MindMap/Quiz etc.

items.name: UI/UX Pro Max
items.summary: A multi-agent UI/UX design intelligence skill and installer with searchable design databases for styles, color palettes, typography, UX rules, chart guidance, product-specific reasoning, and stack-specific implementation advice.
use_cases: Generate project-specific UI design systems with product, style, color, typography, landing-page pattern, and anti-pattern recommendations.
Guide UI implementation and review for landing pages, SaaS apps, dashboards, admin panels, mobile apps, e-commerce, portfolios, and component libraries.
Search structured UI/UX knowledge across styles, palettes, typography, chart types, UX guidelines, and framework-specific best practices.
Install a UI/UX skill into Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Continue, GitHub Copilot, and other AI coding assistants.
Persist generated design systems into a project-local design-system folder for later AI-agent retrieval.

links.note: Project homepage linked from README and skill metadata.
Overview, feature list, supported platforms, install commands, and usage examples.
Main Claude skill file reviewed in the repository.
Cross-platform skill metadata with version, supported platforms, and install command.

risk_notes: Not installed or executed locally as of 2026-06-17.
Installer commands can write skill files into project-local or global AI assistant directories; global installs may affect future agent sessions across projects.
The npm package version checked was 2.2.3 while the repository metadata and tag show 2.5.0; verify current npm and GitHub release state before installing.
Repository root LICENSE and package.json report MIT, but cli/README.md says CC-BY-NC-4.0; resolve the license inconsistency before redistributing or adapting assets.
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json still reports 2.2.1 while skill.json and plugin.json report 2.5.0; treat marketplace metadata as potentially stale.
The CLI can fetch GitHub releases in legacy/update flows or generate files from bundled assets; review the installed output in a disposable project first.
Generated skill content includes design rules that can strongly shape UI decisions and may conflict with an existing product design system.
The search script can persist design-system/MASTER.md and page override files into the current project when --persist is used.
The repository includes many CSV databases, scripts, templates, TTF font assets, and multiple Claude skill directories; review licensing and install footprint before active use.
Search and generated recommendations are rule/database driven; outputs still need human review for accessibility, brand fit, and implementation correctness.

source_review.github_api_status: GitHub API returned 403 during review; git ls-remote, shallow clone, raw repository files, and npm registry metadata were used.
source_review.license: MIT in root LICENSE and package metadata; cli/README.md says CC-BY-NC-4.0.
